title: Lingnania remotiflora
taxonomy: Lingnania remotiflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae, which is a group of parasitic plants commonly known as mistletoes. This family is further divided into several genera, with Lingnania being one of the lesser-known ones. The Loranthaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, with Lingnania remotiflora specifically found in Southeast Asia. As a member of this family, Lingnania remotiflora has a unique parasitic lifestyle, obtaining its nutrients by attaching to the roots of host plants. Its classification is significant in understanding the evolutionary relationships within the Loranthaceae family and the adaptations of its species.
